triage_flags.note: mana_cost '{2}{G}' present. green-magic and mono-green already in tags_filter. No triage issues.

trivia_bullets.text: Spineseeker Centipede functions as a dual-role common: an enters-the-battlefield land-tutor (searches library for a basic land, puts it into hand) combined with a Delirium pump that grows it to a 3/3 with vigilance when four or more card types are in the graveyard. This makes it unusually efficient for lower-powered formats as both a mana fixer and a late-game threat. [Scryfall]

Spineseeker Centipede appears in approximately 5,413 Commander decks on EDHREC — notably high for a common creature. Its top commander homes include Grist, the Hunger Tide; Zask, Skittering Swarmlord; and The Swarmweaver — all Insect-tribal or graveyard-value commanders where both the ramp ETB and Delirium body are relevant. [EDHREC: Spineseeker Centipede, via WebSearch snippet]

Duskmourn: House of Horror was released September 27, 2024, as the 102nd Magic expansion. The set is set entirely within a single demonic haunted mansion ruled by Valgavoth, inspired by 1970s-1990s horror cinema including slasher films, The Amityville Horror, and found-footage films. The Insect creature type here reflects the house's undergrowth and biological decay horror subgenre — vermin and centipedes as natural horror imagery. [Wizards of the Coast: First Look at Duskmourn, via WebSearch snippet]
sources: Wizards of the Coast: First Look at Duskmourn (via WebSearch snippet)

trivia_bullets.text: Spineseeker Centipede was illustrated by Dave Kendall, a British illustrator whose MTG portfolio spans over 154 card credits. Kendall is known for visceral, horror-oriented MTG art — prior credits include Abhorrent Overlord, Bladestitched Skaab, and Diregraf Ghoul. His personal statement identifies horror as his primary artistic passion. The centipede's iridescent pink-magenta palette is unusual for green Insect creatures and gives the card an immediately recognizable visual identity. [artofmtg.com: Dave Kendall, via WebSearch snippet]
sources: Gatherer: Dave Kendall artist page (via WebSearch snippet)
